english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Titus

verses.latin_vulgate: Admone illos principibus, et potestatibus subditos esse, dicto obedire, ad omne opus bonum paratos esse:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπομίμνῃσκε αὐτοὺς ἀρχαῖς καὶ ἐξουσίαις ὑποτάσσεσθαι πειθαρχεῖν πρὸς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἑτοίμους εἶναι
verses.king_james: Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,

verses.latin_vulgate: neminem blasphemare, non litigiosos esse, sed modestos, omnem ostendentes mansuetudinem ad omnes homines.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδένα βλασφημεῖν ἀμάχους εἶναι ἐπιεικεῖς πᾶσαν ἐνδεικνυμένους πρᾳότητα πρὸς πάντας ἀνθρώπους
verses.king_james: To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, *but* gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eramus enim aliquando et nos insipientes, increduli, errantes, servientes desideriis, et voluptatibus variis, in malitia et invidia agentes, odibiles, odientes invicem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦμεν γάρ ποτε καὶ ἡμεῖς ἀνόητοι ἀπειθεῖς πλανώμενοι δουλεύοντες ἐπιθυμίαις καὶ ἡδοναῖς ποικίλαις ἐν κακίᾳ καὶ φθόνῳ διάγοντες στυγητοί μισοῦντες ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, *and* hating one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem benignitas et humanitas apparuit Salvatoris nostri Dei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἡ χρηστότης καὶ ἡ φιλανθρωπία ἐπεφάνη τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,

verses.latin_vulgate: non ex operibus justitiæ, quæ fecimus nos, sed secundum suam misericordiam salvos nos fecit per lavacrum regenerationis et renovationis Spiritus Sancti,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων τῶν ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ ὧν ἐποιήσαμεν ἡμεῖς ἀλλὰ κατὰ τὸν αὐτοῦ ἔλεον ἔσωσεν ἡμᾶς διὰ λουτροῦ παλιγγενεσίας καὶ ἀνακαινώσεως πνεύματος ἁγίου
verses.king_james: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;

verses.latin_vulgate: quem effudit in nos abunde per Jesum Christum Salvatorem nostrum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗ ἐξέχεεν ἐφ᾽ ἡμᾶς πλουσίως διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut justificati gratia ipsius, hæredes simus secundum spem vitæ æternæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα δικαιωθέντες τῇ ἐκείνου χάριτι κληρονόμοι γενώμεθα κατ᾽ ἐλπίδα ζωῆς αἰωνίου
verses.king_james: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fidelis sermo est: et de his volo te confirmare: ut curent bonis operibus præesse qui credunt Deo. Hæc sunt bona, et utilia hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος καὶ περὶ τούτων βούλομαί σε διαβεβαιοῦσθαι ἵνα φροντίζωσιν καλῶν ἔργων προΐστασθαι οἱ πεπιστευκότες τῷ θεῷ ταῦτά ἐστιν τὰ καλὰ καὶ ὠφέλιμα τοῖς ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: *This is* a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultas autem quæstiones, et genealogias, et contentiones, et pugnas legis devita: sunt enim inutiles, et vanæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μωρὰς δὲ ζητήσεις καὶ γενεαλογίας καὶ ἔρεις καὶ μάχας νομικὰς περιΐστασο εἰσὶν γὰρ ἀνωφελεῖς καὶ μάταιοι
verses.king_james: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæreticum hominem post unam et secundam correptionem devita:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱρετικὸν ἄνθρωπον μετὰ μίαν καὶ δευτέραν νουθεσίαν παραιτοῦ
verses.king_james: A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;

verses.latin_vulgate: sciens quia subversus est, qui ejusmodi est, et delinquit, cum sit proprio judicio condemnatus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδὼς ὅτι ἐξέστραπται ὁ τοιοῦτος καὶ ἁμαρτάνει ὢν αὐτοκατάκριτος
verses.king_james: Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum misero ad te Artemam, aut Tychicum, festina ad me venire Nicopolim: ibi enim statui hiemare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν πέμψω ἀρτεμᾶν πρὸς σὲ ἢ τυχικόν σπούδασον ἐλθεῖν πρός με εἰς νικόπολιν ἐκεῖ γὰρ κέκρικα παραχειμάσαι
verses.king_james: When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Zenam legisperitum et Apollo sollicite præmitte, ut nihil illis desit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ζηνᾶν τὸν νομικὸν καὶ ἀπολλῶ σπουδαίως πρόπεμψον ἵνα μηδὲν αὐτοῖς λείπῃ
verses.king_james: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Discant autem et nostri bonis operibus præesse ad usus necessarios: ut non sint infructuosi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μανθανέτωσαν δὲ καὶ οἱ ἡμέτεροι καλῶν ἔργων προΐστασθαι εἰς τὰς ἀναγκαίας χρείας ἵνα μὴ ὦσιν ἄκαρποι
verses.king_james: And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutant te qui mecum sunt omnes: saluta eos qui nos amant in fide. Gratia Dei cum omnibus vobis. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονταί σε οἱ μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ πάντες ἄσπασαι τοὺς φιλοῦντας ἡμᾶς ἐν πίστει ἡ χάρις μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace *be* with you all. Amen.
